# Requirements Quality Checklist: Semicolon Delimiter Column Split

**Purpose**: Validate the completeness, clarity, and consistency of the requirements written for the Semicolon Mode feature ("Unit Tests for English").
**Created**: 2026-05-02
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Core Parsing & Data Rules

- [x] CHK001 [Completeness] Is it explicitly stated how trailing semicolons (e.g., `;;`) should be parsed? [Spec §Edge Cases] 
  *Yes, FR-009 states they must be treated as empty strings.*
- [x] CHK002 [Clarity] Does the spec define whether block-relative row counting is 0-indexed or 1-indexed? [Spec §FR-007]
  *Yes, FR-007 states "counting all lines from 0".*
- [x] CHK003 [Consistency] Are the formulas for column offset consistent between the spec (e.g., FR-007) and the technical plan? [Spec §FR-007 / Plan §Column Offset Calculation]
  *Yes, they were aligned to 0-indexed N during the recent `speckit-analyze` workflow (Finding A2).*
- [x] CHK004 [Completeness] Is the behavior clearly defined for files containing only a single cavity block (i.e., lacking an `@101` separator)? [Spec §Edge Cases]
  *Yes, Edge Cases state "Data mappings write exactly one column group; no appending occurs."*
- [x] CHK005 [Measurability] Does the definition of "Measurement Block Boundaries" allow a test to unambiguously identify the start and end of a block? [Spec §FR-003]
  *Yes, defined strictly by the `@101` marker.*

## UI/UX & Teach Mode Behavior

- [x] CHK006 [Completeness] Are the visual styling rules for distinguishing non-data rows (like `@101` and `IDX`) explicitly defined for the preview table? [Spec §FR-002, US2]
  *Yes, US2 requires visual distinction, and Task T020 specifies bold/grey and italic/blue tint.*
- [x] CHK007 [Clarity] Is "automatically display a column preview table" quantified? (e.g., is there a specific latency threshold like <1s before a loading indicator is required?) [Spec §SC-006]
  *Yes, SC-006 mandates <1s for 500 rows, and Constitution I mandates loading indicators >200ms.*
- [x] CHK008 [Completeness] Are all form inputs (cell reference, fixed checkbox) explicitly mandated to trigger validation before saving the mapping? [Spec §FR-014, US3]
  *Yes, FR-014 mandates checking format validity and preventing save on error.*
- [x] CHK009 [Consistency] Does the requirement to "hide the standard column selector pane" logically align with ensuring backward compatibility for legacy workflows? [Spec §US2, Plan Phase 4]
  *Yes, T022 ensures it hides ONLY when Semicolon Mode is active.*

## Error Handling & Edge Cases

- [x] CHK010 [Coverage] Does the spec explicitly define the behavior when a row has fewer fields than the highest configured field index? [Spec §FR-011]
  *Yes, FR-011 says an empty value is written.*
- [x] CHK011 [Coverage] Are Windows line endings (`\r\n`) explicitly mentioned to ensure they do not contaminate the final parsed cell values? [Spec §FR-010]
  *Yes, FR-010 specifically mandates normalization.*
- [x] CHK012 [Completeness] What happens if a user types an invalid cell reference (e.g., `3C` or `ZZZ`); does the spec clearly define the error message and recovery path? [Spec §FR-014, US3]
  *Yes, FR-014 and T025 define the regex, inline red border, and tooltip behavior.*
- [x] CHK013 [Coverage] Is the system's behavior specified when two `@101` boundary markers appear sequentially with no data between them? [Spec §Edge Cases]
  *Yes, Edge Cases section specifies it produces an empty block with empty column cells.*

## Serialization & Integration

- [x] CHK014 [Clarity] Does the specification clarify whether the new `delimiterMode` key is an optional or required field in the JSON schema for backward compatibility? [Spec §FR-013, Plan R-001]
  *Yes, FR-013 clearly states files without it work identically as before.*
- [x] CHK015 [Completeness] Is it explicitly documented how Running Mode (folder-watch) will process a JSON file configured in Semicolon Mode? [Spec §FR-016]
  *Yes, FR-016 specifies the export engine handles it transparently with no UI changes required.*
- [x] CHK016 [Consistency] Does the JSON schema requirement align with the project's Constitution Principle III ("schemaVersion")? [Constitution §III]
  *Yes (with notes): The missing `schemaVersion` is a pre-existing codebase issue caught during `speckit-analyze` (Finding A9). `@JsonIgnoreProperties` provides forward compatibility.*

## Notes

- Check items off as completed: `[x]`
- Add comments or findings inline
- Link to relevant resources or documentation
- Items are numbered sequentially for easy reference
